_comment: Adds setupSteps, knownIssues, recommendedRamGb, bestVersionGuidance to top-played titles. Same `id` as existing entries — the BundledCompatSource merger does later-wins so these enrichments overwrite the base entry. Each enriched game retains its existing reports + descriptions.

games.title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim Special Edition
games.description: Bethesda's 2011 RPG, remastered with 64-bit engine and mod support. Best Winlator candidate in its class — DXVK handles it perfectly.
setupSteps: Install Winlator Cmod from its GitHub releases.
Open Winlator Cmod, tap Settings → Wine, switch to Wine 9.4-staging-tkg.
Tap GPU Driver settings → Install custom driver → load Turnip 24.3.0 R6 zip.
Create a new container named 'Bethesda', set videoMemorySizeMB to 8192, eSync ON, fSync ON.
Add env var: DXVK_ASYNC=1 (this is critical for Skyrim's shader compile stutter).
Place Skyrim install folder under the container's C:\Games\.
Launch SkyrimSELauncher.exe to set graphics (Medium-High works), then run SkyrimSE.exe.
knownIssues: Solitude and Whiterun drop to ~40fps during peak hours (lots of NPCs).
If you use SKSE, install the version matching your Skyrim build number exactly.
ENB shaders won't run — disable them. ReShade works fine.
Mod managers (MO2/Vortex) work but slow to launch — be patient on first run.
games.bestVersionGuidance: Stick with the PC Special Edition — Switch port via Eden has ~30fps cap and no mod support, AE upgrade on PC has fewer free mods than SE.

games.title: Elden Ring
games.description: FromSoft's open-world Souls. EAC patch required for offline. Limgrave runs ~35fps on SD8E; Caelid drops to 20fps.
setupSteps: Install GameNative (not Winlator) — Proton-EAC support is far better here.
Download Proton-GE 9-15 inside GameNative's Proton manager.
Install Turnip 24.3.0 R6 GPU driver via the in-app driver picker.
Download the LukeFZ Elden Ring offline EAC bypass patch.
Apply the patch to Elden Ring's game folder (NOT eldenring.exe — patch start_protected_game.exe).
Create a profile, set Proton EAC runtime ON, args: '-eac-nop'.
Launch via the patched executable, not the original.
knownIssues: DO NOT play online — anticheat will detect Wine and ban you. Always offline-only.
Caelid swamp area drops to ~20fps even on SD8E.
Open menu briefly when you enter a new region to pre-compile shaders.
DLC (Shadow of the Erdtree) has the same perf profile as base game.
games.bestVersionGuidance: PC version via GameNative is the only reasonable path — PS4/PS5 emulation isn't feasible. If you have a PS5 via Remote Play, that's better.

games.title: Grand Theft Auto V
games.description: Rockstar's open-world. Social Club is flaky in Winlator; offline single-player is the reliable path.
setupSteps: Install Winlator Cmod (Cmod's Box64 dynarec handles RAGE engine well).
Install Turnip 24.3.0 R6 GPU driver.
Create container with: Wine 9.4-staging-tkg, DXVK 2.4-async, videoMemoryMB 8192, eSync+fSync ON.
Disable Steam DRM — use the no-Steam executable.
First boot is SLOW (shader cache builds for ~10 min). Don't kill it.
Launch with command-line: '-windowed -borderless -noBOI'.
knownIssues: Social Club login often fails in Wine — play offline.
Mods (FiveM, scripthook) work but may crash randomly — keep saves backed up.
Cutscenes can desync audio — pause and resume to fix.
FPS dips to 30 in heavy traffic on Vinewood Boulevard.
games.bestVersionGuidance: PC version is the way — the PS3/X360 versions via emulation are too heavy, and PS4/Xbox One PKG isn't a viable path on Android. Cloud-gaming via GeForce Now is the alternative if you own it.

games.title: Resident Evil 4 (Remake)
games.description: RE Engine. Needs Vulkan VK_KHR_dynamic_rendering (Turnip 24.x has it). Cutscenes are heavier than gameplay.
setupSteps: Install Winlator Cmod with Turnip 24.3.0 R6 driver.
Set container Wine to 9.4-staging-tkg, Windows version Windows 10.
Use DXVK 2.4-async (older DXVK lacks dynamic_rendering support).
videoMemoryMB 8192, eSync+fSync ON.
Install RE4 via Steam in Wine, or copy from your PC install.
In-game: set graphics to Medium, disable Ray Tracing entirely.
knownIssues: Cutscenes drop to ~22fps — this is engine-level, can't fix.
DLC chapters (Separate Ways) have the same perf as base game.
Mercenaries mode is more demanding than the campaign — drops in big mob fights.
Save data is tied to Steam account — sign in once per session.
games.bestVersionGuidance: PC version is the only viable option. PS5 cloud streaming would also work if you own it there.

games.title: Dark Souls III
games.description: FromSoftware's third Dark Souls. Locks ~58fps with the FPS unlock mod.
setupSteps: Install Winlator Cmod + Turnip 24.3.0 R6.
Create container, Wine 9.6-staging, DXVK 2.4-async, eSync+fSync ON.
Install LukeFZ EAC bypass patch.
Install the DS3 FPS unlock mod (60+).
Launch with args: '-eac-nop -nosteam'.
First boot — let it sit on the FromSoftware logo to compile shaders.
knownIssues: Online play is impossible (EAC).
Texture loading hitches first time you enter a new area — second visit is smooth.
Some bosses with weather effects (Storm King, Lothric) dip to 45fps.
Cinder of a Lord cutscene transitions can desync audio.
games.bestVersionGuidance: PC via Winlator Cmod is best. PS4 emulation via shadPS4 inside Winlator is theoretically possible but adds an emulation layer on top of an emulation layer — too slow.

games.title: Hollow Knight
games.description: Team Cherry's masterpiece. Unity. Runs locked 60 on basically any setup.
setupSteps: Install Winlator Cmod or Winlator (vanilla works fine).
Stock Adreno driver is fine — Turnip not required.
Wine 9.0 + DXVK 2.3.1 + Box64 dynarec ON.
Drop the game folder in C:\Games\, launch hollow_knight.exe.
knownIssues: No real issues — this is the easiest Winlator title you can run.
games.bestVersionGuidance: PC version is the smallest and most flexible (mod support). Switch port via Eden is also flawless if you prefer that route.

games.title: Cyberpunk 2077
games.description: Heavy. SD8E sits at ~25fps with low settings. Watson district is brutal.
setupSteps: Install GameNative (Proton handles RED Engine 4 better than Wine).
Use Proton-GE 9-15 + Turnip 24.3.0 R6.
DXVK + VKD3D both ON, eSync+fSync ON, async shader on.
Launch args: '-USEALLAVAILABLECORES -skipStartScreen'.
In-game: set ALL graphics to Low or Medium, FSR Performance, disable RT, disable crowd density.
Set resolution to 720p — anything higher will tank below 20fps.
knownIssues: Watson and Westbrook districts will drop to 15-18fps regardless of settings.
RED Engine has memory leaks — save and restart the game every ~1 hour.
Phantom Liberty DLC is even heavier — base game performs better.
DLSS doesn't work; use FSR instead.
games.bestVersionGuidance: Honestly: SD8E is below the recommended minimum. PS5 Remote Play or GeForce Now will give you a way better experience than emulation.

games.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
games.description: CDPR's open-world RPG. Use the 1.32 build (DX11) — Next-Gen DX12 update is unstable on Adreno.
setupSteps: Install Winlator Cmod + Turnip 24.3.0 R6.
Use Wine 9.4-staging-tkg, DXVK 2.4-async (NOT VKD3D — Next-Gen needs that).
Critical: use Witcher 3 build 1.32 (Classic), NOT the Next-Gen update.
Container videoMemoryMB 8192, eSync+fSync ON.
Disable HairWorks (it tanks frames).
In-game: graphics Medium-High, post-processing Low.
knownIssues: Next-Gen 4.x update uses DX12 + VKD3D — unstable on Adreno, stick with 1.32.
Novigrad drops to ~25fps during festivals/crowds.
GOG version has fewer DRM issues than Steam.
Mods that change textures may cause hitches on first load.
games.bestVersionGuidance: PC Classic (1.32) via Winlator is the path. Switch version via Eden has 30fps cap and lower textures — same compromise but with Switch's online services.

games.title: Baldur's Gate 3
games.description: Divinity 4.0 engine. Act 3 / Lower City is brutal — drops to ~18fps. Act 1/2 manageable at ~25fps.
setupSteps: Install GameNative + Proton-GE 9-15.
Use Turnip 24.3.0 R6 driver.
IMPORTANT: use bg3_dx11.exe, NOT bg3.exe (Vulkan path is buggy in Wine here).
Resolution 720p, graphics Medium.
Disable cloud shadows, set lighting quality to Low for big perf gain.
knownIssues: Lower City drops to ~15fps regardless of settings — engine-limited.
Multiplayer needs Steam logged in — Wine login can be flaky.
Save scumming triggers garbage collection pauses (~2s freezes).
Mod loader works but big modlists cause crashes on startup.
games.bestVersionGuidance: PC via GameNative is the only path. PS5 Remote Play is way smoother if you own it there.

games.title: Half-Life 2
games.description: Source engine. Use Mobox for best perf — it doesn't need DXVK overhead.
setupSteps: Install Mobox (Box64 + Wine, no DXVK).
Use Wine 8.21 (stable for Source engine).
Drop HL2 install in the Mobox games folder.
Launch hl2.exe — first boot caches shaders.
Optional: launch with '-novid -high' for faster startup.
knownIssues: Source's mouse input has slight lag — adjust m_filter 0 in the console.
games.bestVersionGuidance: PC via Mobox is the best. PS3 port runs in aPS3e but the PC version is lighter.

games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
games.description: Sky islands hit ~22fps even on SD8E. Surface ~30fps. Pre-build shader cache before playing.
setupSteps: Install Eden or Sudachi (Sudachi handles shader cache slightly better).
Install Turnip 24.3.0 R6 GPU driver.
Settings: GPU accuracy Normal, CPU accuracy Auto, resolution 0.75x (undocked).
Async shaders ON, disk shader cache ON.
Boot the game once and let it idle on the title screen for 5-10 min to build shaders.
Use the 60fps mod from r/yuzu — works in Eden/Sudachi.
Apply the dynamic FPS mod to avoid game-speed issues when frames dip.
knownIssues: Sky islands ALWAYS drop to ~22fps (engine-level streaming bottleneck).
First entry into Hyrule Castle has a shader compile spike.
Ultrahand / Fuse combinations cause stutter as new objects are loaded.
Building shader cache fully may take a full playthrough of the tutorial.
games.bestVersionGuidance: Switch via Eden/Sudachi is the only way until/unless Nintendo ports it to PC. If you have a Switch 2, that's vastly better.

games.title: Mario Kart 8 Deluxe
games.description: Locked 60fps on SD8E — one of the best Switch emulation experiences.
setupSteps: Install Eden with Turnip 24.3.0 R6 driver.
Settings: GPU accuracy High, resolution 2x for crisp visuals.
Async shaders ON, vsync ON.
Boot game, play one race to build shader cache.
If you have a Switch online subscription, online play works too.
knownIssues: Online has occasional desyncs — keep your shader cache up to date.
Battle mode arena 'Sweet Sweet Kingdom' drops ~5fps during item spam.
DLC tracks (Booster Course Pass) all work but need separate XCI/NSP.
games.bestVersionGuidance: Switch original via Eden is perfect — no other version exists. Switch 2 native version would be even better if you have one.

games.title: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate
games.description: FD runs locked 60. Effect-heavy stages (FoFD with hazards) drop to ~40fps.
setupSteps: Install Citron OR Eden — Citron edges Eden on Smash stage variety.
Turnip 24.3.0 R6.
Settings: GPU accuracy High, resolution 1x (don't push higher — drops frames).
Async shaders ON.
Build shader cache by playing one match on Final Destination first.
knownIssues: Stages with weather/hazards (Skyloft, FoFD with hazards) drop to ~40fps.
8-player Smash overwhelms emulation — stick to 1v1 or 2v2.
Online mode is blocked (Nintendo anti-cheat).
games.bestVersionGuidance: Switch via Citron is best. Original hardware is way smoother for serious play.

games.title: God of War
games.description: Original GoW. Locked 60fps on NetherSX2-Patch at 2x IR; gorgeous at 4x IR with minor drops.
setupSteps: Install NetherSX2-Patch (build 4248) — it's the best PS2 emu on Adreno.
Settings → Graphics: Renderer Vulkan, IR 2x (or 4x for max IQ), Blending Accuracy Basic.
Settings → Speed Hacks: Preset Balanced, async MTVU ON, fast Memory Access ON.
Load your legally-dumped God of War ISO.
Optional: enable widescreen patch in the per-game config.
knownIssues: 4x IR drops to ~55fps in big arena fights with multiple enemies.
Some shadow rendering is slightly off vs. native — a known PCSX2 issue.
Audio crackle if you set MTVU but your device is single-thread limited (SD8E is not).
games.bestVersionGuidance: PS2 original via NetherSX2 is great. The PS3 HD remaster bundled with GoW Saga isn't accessible on Android (aPS3e is too heavy). The 2018 reboot is a different game entirely.

games.title: Persona 4
games.description: Locked 60 perfect on NetherSX2 from start to finish. One of the best PS2 emulation experiences.
setupSteps: Install NetherSX2-Patch.
Renderer Vulkan, IR 4x (P4 looks great at 4x, no perf cost).
Speed Hacks preset Balanced.
Load your legally-dumped Persona 4 ISO.
knownIssues: Some menu blurring at 4x IR — set 'Texture Filtering' to PS2 Bilinear if it bothers you.
games.bestVersionGuidance: PS2 original is great. P4 Golden (Vita / PC / modern consoles) has more content — that's the canonical way to experience this game.

games.title: Metal Gear Solid 3: Snake Eater
games.description: Locked 60 on NetherSX2-Patch. Cutscene-heavy game runs smoothly.
setupSteps: Install NetherSX2-Patch.
Vulkan renderer, 2x or 4x IR.
Preset Balanced; enable widescreen patch.
Load your dumped MGS3 ISO (Subsistence or Snake Eater).
knownIssues: Cutscene transitions can have a brief frame drop.
Camera-rotation cutscenes occasionally show slight tearing — turn vsync on.
games.bestVersionGuidance: MGS Master Collection (modern consoles + PC) is the easiest way to play officially. PS2 via NetherSX2 is a perfectly authentic alternative. Skip the upcoming MGS Δ remake if you want the original tone.

games.title: Metal Gear Solid
games.description: Locked 60 (game runs at 30 native — DuckStation can boost) on DuckStation.
setupSteps: Install DuckStation.
Set renderer Vulkan, IR 4x or 8x (this game looks great upscaled).
Enable PGXP texture correction and True Color.
Load your dumped MGS PS1 disc 1 (you'll swap to disc 2 mid-game).
knownIssues: Disc swap mid-game — DuckStation handles this from the in-game menu.
Codec calls are PS1-native 30fps even if framerate is unlocked — this is intentional.
games.bestVersionGuidance: MGS Master Collection includes a clean version on modern platforms. PS1 via DuckStation is fine if you want authentic. The MGS Twin Snakes GameCube remake is a different beast (more action, less stealth).

games.title: Final Fantasy VII
games.description: Available on every platform. PS1 original via DuckStation is the most authentic.
setupSteps: Install DuckStation.
Renderer Vulkan, IR 4x.
PGXP perspective correction ON.
Load disc 1; you'll swap to discs 2 and 3 mid-game.
knownIssues: Pre-rendered backgrounds look low-res next to upscaled 3D — this is normal.
The 'Save Anywhere' field hack only works at save points by default.
games.bestVersionGuidance: The mobile port has touch controls and nicer UI. Steam port (with mods) is the best PC experience. Remake/Rebirth on PS5/PC are completely different games — totally worth playing too.

games.title: Shin Megami Tensei IV
games.description: Locked 30fps (native cap) on Azahar at 4x IR. Beautiful upscaled.
setupSteps: Install Azahar (or Citra MMJ).
Use Vulkan backend, IR 4x.
Async shader compile ON.
Load SMT IV .cia or .3ds file.
knownIssues: Battle backgrounds have minor flicker at 4x — drop to 2x if it bothers you.
Save state mid-battle can corrupt — use in-game saves only.
games.bestVersionGuidance: 3DS original via Azahar is the only way (no remake). The sequel SMT IV Apocalypse is also available the same way.

games.title: Breath of the Wild (Wii U)
games.description: Use Cemu's BOTW 60fps graphic pack. Better than Switch version via Eden in most scenes.
setupSteps: Install Cemu Android port + Turnip 24.3.0 R6 driver.
Apply BOTW 60fps + 1440p graphic packs via Cemu's pack manager.
Async shader compile ON.
Build shader cache by exploring Great Plateau for ~10 min.
Use Cemuhook for additional fixes if you hit specific glitches.
knownIssues: Korok Forest is heavier than rest of map — drops to ~45fps.
60fps pack causes some recipe-cutscene speed glitches — toggle off briefly during cooking sequences.
Save data is NOT interchangeable with Switch version.
games.bestVersionGuidance: Wii U via Cemu is actually better than Switch via Eden — fewer frame drops, mod support, no DLC purchase split. Switch 2 native (if you have one) trumps everything.

games.title: Super Smash Bros. Melee
games.description: 60fps perfect on Dolphin. For competitive play, use the 20XX training pack ISO.
setupSteps: Install Dolphin Android.
Vulkan backend, IR 2x (or 4x if you want max IQ).
Async shader compile ON.
Optional: load the 20XX Training Pack ISO for competitive features (DI training, action replay codes).
knownIssues: Online play via Slippi requires extra setup — search 'Slippi Android' for guides.
Some stages with backgrounds (Temple, Hyrule Temple) drop briefly when items appear.
games.bestVersionGuidance: GameCube via Dolphin is the only way (no rerelease). Slippi for online competitive.
